chapter.label: Tale 120
chapter.title: Three Dead Monks Found By Fishermen At Paran

paragraphs.text: Once we went to Bouchri, at the other side of the Red Sea. We took a good catch and made our way back. We anchored off Pteleos, wanting to sail on to Raithou, but we were detained by contrary winds, A heavy sea held us up for ninety days. Venturiag into that great wilderness, we found three anchorites dead under one stone.
[They wore habits of palm-fibre and their cloaks were lying beside them. We brought the three corpses to the ship and at once the stormy sea subsided. The winds shifted into a favourable direction.
We sailed with a following wind and came to Raithou. The fathers buried them, together with the elders of former times.]
